name: Parque Nacional de Monfragüe — circular por la Vía Verde del Monfragüe
region_intro: Touring cycling in Monfragüe offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Tajo and Tiétar rivers, which carve through canyons and create impressive geological formations. The region features a Mediterranean forest and scrubland ecosystem, with cork and holm oaks, alongside steep hills. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, including paved roads, paths, and sections of compacted gravel, providing routes for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes in Monfragüe The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Nacional de Monfragüe — circular por la Vía Verde del Monfragüe, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route navigates through the park's Mediterranean forest and river valleys. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Torrejón el Rubio from the Tejo River – circular through Monfragüe National Park, a difficult 25.0 miles (40.2 km) path. It offers views of the Tajo River and the diverse scenery within the National Park. Local touring cyclists also love the Tour of Monfragüe National Park, a 67.0 miles (107.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and steep hills, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Touring cycling in Monfragüe is defined by Mediterranean forest, river valleys, and steep hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding climbs to winding descents. The routes in Monfragüe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Monfragüe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Monfragüe?
A: Monfragüe offers a good selection of touring cycling routes, with a total of 25 routes documented on komoot. These range in difficulty from easy to difficult, catering to various experience levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Monfragüe?
A: You'll encounter diverse terrain, including paved roads, state roads, paths, and streets. Some sections may feature compacted gravel or unpaved surfaces. The landscape is characterized by Mediterranean forests, scrubland, and dramatic features shaped by the Tajo and Tiétar rivers, often involving demanding climbs and winding descents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Monfragüe?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for all fitness levels. For instance, the "Panoramic Monfragüe" route (not listed in the top 5, but mentioned in region research) combines tarmac and dirt roads in good condition. Generally, komoot lists 1 easy route and 7 moderate routes, which might be suitable for families or beginners looking for less challenging rides.

Q: What are the best times of year to go touring cycling in Monfragüe?
A: The best times are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. In warmer months, it's highly recommended to cycle at dawn to avoid the heat and increase your chances of spotting wildlife near the rivers. The autumn rutting season for deer is also a notable event.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while cycling in Monfragüe?
A: Monfragüe is a birdwatching paradise, especially for raptors. You can frequently spot griffon vultures, black vultures, Spanish imperial eagles, and black storks soaring above the cliffs. Besides birds, the park is home to deer, wild boar, foxes, and otters, particularly near the rivers.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Monfragüe?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular tours. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185231895/parque-nacional-de-monfraguee-circular-por-la-via-verde-del-monfraguee" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Monfragüe — circular por la Vía Verde del Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt;, a 71.3 km trail that navigates through the park's Mediterranean forest and river valleys.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Monfragüe is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can visit the 9th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, or the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Gitano (Gypsy's Leap)&lt;/a&gt;, a 300-meter-high cliff ideal for birdwatching. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrano Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/808762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find visitor information or services within Monfragüe National Park?
A: The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4458065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarreal de San Carlos&lt;/a&gt;, located within the park, houses an interpretation and visitor center. This is a good place to start your exploration and gather more information about the area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Monfragüe?
A: The touring cycling routes in Monfragüe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the opportunities for wildlife observation, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Monfragüe offers several difficult routes for experienced cyclists. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185251889/tour-of-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 107.8 km trail with over 1300 meters of elevation gain, typically completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105601593/torrejon-el-rubio-from-the-tejo-river-circular-through-monfraguee-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón el Rubio from the Tejo River – circular through Monfragüe National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a 40.2 km difficult path offering views of the Tajo River.

Q: Is there a route that follows the Tajo River through the park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185297944/tajo-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a touring cycling route that winds through the park, offering scenic views along the Tagus River. This route is 26.4 km long and is rated as difficult.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous Salto del Gitano?
A: Yes, a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap – Villarreal de San Carlos loop from Villarreal de San Carlos&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes this iconic viewpoint. This 33.5 km difficult route offers stunning views of the Tagus River gorge.
tips: Here you can watch the vultures very well. Definitely take a good pair of binoculars with you. It's fascinating when the vultures glide through the air using the thermals.
Small settlement to pause. There is an information film about the region and information about the native birds.
This viewpoint is ideal for birdwatching, especially birds of prey and cliff-dwelling birds. It's also surrounded by lush vegetation.
This greenway explores the beautiful landscape of the Extremaduran pastureland. A winding ecosystem where holm oaks reign supreme and cork oaks grow lushly, creating a perfect refuge for wildlife.
On the Tagus Nature Trail (GR-113) and next to the Tiétar river you can find a large number of viewpoints with beautiful views of the Monfragüe National Park and this is one of them.
